FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Transmittal of Correspondence
(Rev. 7/87)

Correspondent                                                                      Advice # 
[illegible] Dale &                                                              37-311
Agency/City
[illegible] City, City Attn
Subject
[illegible] staff late
Date Received 2/11/87 27  Add'l Info Reqstd ____  Due Date 1/12/08

Request SEI  ☐  21-day letter sent on 12/11/87  CC to:_____________

                                        Assigned To

                    Margarita A.  ☐              Bob L.        ☐
                    Diane         ☐              John Mc       ☐
                    Jeanette      ☐              Kathy         ☐
                    Lilly         ☐              TA&A          ☐

Comments:________________________________________________________________

_______________________________________________________________________

_______________________________________________________________________

LAW OFFICES

CHRISTENSEN, BACIGALUPI & MONTOY
AN ASSOCIATION OF ATTORNEYS
6067 NORTH FRESNO STREET, SUITE 101
FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93710

CRAIG G. CHRISTENSEN
DALE E. BACIGALUPI*
HILDA CANTU MONTOY
LISA M. GAMOIAN

TELEPHONE: (209) 431-6800
TELECOPIER: (209) 431-4216

*A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

December 7, 1987

California Fair Political Practices
Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Linda Moureaux

Re: Your Letter of December 4, 1987

Dear Ms. Moureaux:

I enclose herewith a copy of your letter to me dated
December 4, 1987 regarding the annual filing of Statement of
Economic Interests, due April 1, 1987, in connection with my
current office as city attorney for the City of Firebaugh.

Please be advised that as of April 1, 1987 I was not the
city attorney of the City of Firebaugh nor was I the city
attorney for the City of Firebaugh during calendar year 1986.

The City Council appointed me city attorney in Firebaugh on
May 18, 1987.

The city attorney prior to my appointment for several years
was attorney Richard Hargrove who maintains his offices in
Fresno.

Please advise me in writing, before January 4, 1988 whether
I was obliged to meet the April 1, 1987 deadline under the
foregoing circumstances.

Very truly yours,

DALE E. BACIGALUPI

DEB/hm
CC. Clerk, City of Firebaugh

December 4, 1987

Dale Batchigalupi
City Attorney
6067 North Fresno Street, Suite 101
Fresno, CA 93710

RE: Statement of Economic Interests
Type of Statement: Annual
Date Statement Due: April 1, 1987

Dear Mr. Batchigalupi:

The Fair Political Practices Commission has not received
your Statement of Economic Interests which was due on the date
indicated above.

Government Code Section 91013 imposes a fine of $10 per
day up to a maximum of $100 for each day a statement is late.
However, if you file your Statement within 30 days of receipt of
this letter and submit a good reason for filing late, the
Commission can waive all or part of the fine. The fine cannot
be waived if your Statement is not filed within 30 days of
receipt of this letter.

The enclosed 721 Form should be completed as soon as
possible and returned to your agency's filing official who will
make and retain a copy and forward the original to the Fair
Political Practices Commission.

If you have any questions regarding your filing obligation
or need assistance in completing your Statement, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Linda Moureaux
Legal Documents Examiner
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Enclosure
cc: Firebaugh City Clerk

CERTIFIED MAIL-RETURN RECEIPT REQUESTED

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 11, 1987


Dale E. Bacigalupi
Firebaugh City Attorney
Christensen, Bacigalupi & Montoy
6067 North Fresno Street, Suite 101
Fresno, CA 93710

Re: 87-311

Dear Mr. Bacigalupi:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on December 11, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

/Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Dale E. Bacigalupi was obligated to file a Statement of Economic Interests by April 1, 1987, given that he was not appointed as City Attorney of Firebaugh until May 18, 1987, and therefore did not hold the office on the due date or during calendar year 1986.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document package contains multiple items: (1) FPPC transmittal form dated 1987, (2) incoming request from Bacigalupi dated December 7, 1987, (3) FPPC notice of non-receipt dated December 4, 1987, and (4) FPPC acknowledgment of advice request dated December 11, 1987 (reference 87-311). The primary document analyzed is the incoming request letter. No formal FPPC advice/conclusion has yet been issued in this package.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Dale E. Bacigalupi, newly appointed City Attorney of Firebaugh, responding to an FPPC notice that his annual Statement of Economic Interests was not received by the April 1, 1987 deadline. Bacigalupi seeks written advice on whether he was obligated to meet this deadline given that he was not appointed to the position until May 18, 1987.
